text: Renaissance of Innovation
32 ABY
Crestian Tarasar
Caspar

ak (NPC), Axel Vichten, Atsvara Tarasar (NPC), Blulurbgloblurb Druze, Cas Mos (NPC), Crestian Tarasar, Elana Tracer, Etiel te Danaan (NPC), Longinus Van Sen (NPC), Lynae Cassius, Nara Rhen, Sirlahn Inglet (NPC), The Union Council
Caspian Union, Galactic Empire (Imperium), Xi Char / Haor Chall Engineering
The Union Council proclaims a Second Renaissance of Innovation anchored by new designs from the Xi Char and Haor Chall Engineering.
Council Chambers - Union Hall: Plaxton: Caspar

The Council Chambers have recently been renovated to better service the needs of the new Caspian Union. This grand hall serves both as the public meeting space for the Union Council as well as doubling as a lecture hall on occasion.

A stage on the far side of the room is occupied by five benches arranged in a semi-circle facing the audience seating. Each bench is constructed in Wansook pine with a hamogoni stain and topped with Vira granite. There are ten place settings on each bench, set widely apart from each other to better accommodate the physiology of some of the non-Humanoid Councillors.
Projectors embedded into the benches allow the Councillors to appear and vote remotely; a particular necessity given the duties of many of the Councillors.

The shape and style of the seating behind the benches differs widely to suit the physiology and preferences of each of the Councillors, though all are limited to a white to grey colour palette to avoid being distracting. A number of place settings lack seats entirely owing both to the actual size of the Union Council currently and the particular physiology of a few of the Councillors.
Behind the benches is an expansive window of Plaxton outside. The fibres of the window can be altered to the touch of a button to become a projection screen.

The seats for the audience ascend up nearly fifty rows up to a projection booth at the top rim, where large lights are affixed. In front of the seats is a single ornately carved podium, of the same hamogoni-stained Wansook pine of the benches, allowing for the audience members to address the Council. On each side there is also a similarly constructed table with three chairs each to allow for more organised hearing and testimony.

%tCrestian had given important speeches before and other important figures had given important speeches at Union Hall, but tonight's was going to be something truly different. This was the announcement of a recent accomplishment or a response to galactic events but a long planned address on an endeavour that had been literally years in the making.%r%tIn preparation for the address the walls of the Council Chambers have been festooned with tapestries depicting some of the most notable designs of Union shipwrights. Eight capital ships feature against the left wall, the Hurricane Fleet Carrier, Valorous Light Carrier, Admiral Roj Battleship, Hood Battlecruiser, Arsenal Heavy Cruiser, Mergansar Heavy Cruiser, Shearwater Light Cruiser, and the Deep Space Vehicle. Eight shuttles and starfighters feature against the right wall, C-706 Solus, L-188 Lakshadwep, L-200 Halcyon, L-202 Hellhawk, Z-96 Headhunter, Z-100 Centurion, Z-105 Harpoon, and Z-110 Hellion. Each tapestry is the work of hundreds of hours of work by skilled artists.%r%tGuests have poured in the Council Chambers from throughout the Union and beyond. Humans and Sarians are the most plentiful in presence. But, the true diversity of the Union is on display with Bith, Devarionians, Duros, Gotals, Klatooinians, Mikassans, Minor Krits, Nikto, Sluissi, Twi'leks, Vodrans, Weequay, Wookiees, and Zabraks all in noticeable numbers. Perhaps most noticeable are the number of Xi Charrians and Yaries present. They are rarely seen on Caspar in large numbers, but tonight they are ubiquitous.

%tIt had been a long time since Axel Vichten had worn his Caspian Naval uniform, and while it might be a bit outdated by modern standards, the man had actually earned this uniform years ago. With rank insignia of Commander along with the insignia of a ship's captain.%r%tMaking his way along with the others into the chambers, Axel is looking about, taking in the tapestries and wondering where he can get some of those made for his own ships. The man may not be considered anywhere amongst the smartest people in the galaxy, but he knew ships, and was one of the more renowned pilots and naval tacticians in the galaxy.%r%tMingling amongst the others, the man was actually being careful to keep his uniform clean. Even his shoes were polished to a high sheen, as were the multiple service medals he wore. "This should be interesting," Axel says to himself, as he looks towards the Xi Charrians.

%tNearly all the members of the Union Council are physically present including the entire Xi Char delegation and all but one member of the Krittish delegation, both often prone to holographic attendance. Only three members are marked as absent, the Councillors of Goshen, Spittsbury, and Vanover. While it is not stated, it is generally understood by those in the know that they were excluded as a security measure to avoid having the whole of the Union's political leadership in one place. With the three Councillors in question being the most dovish members of the Union Council, they have been spared having to fake enthusiasm for military matters.%r%tThe military character of the announcement is clear from more than just the decor. Several members of the Union Council hold ranks in the Caspian Defence Force in addition to or in connexion with their role in the government. Presav Crestian Tarasar can be seen in his uniform as a Sar Admiral and Vice Presav Cas Mos in hers as a Field Marshal. Tonight the Union Council includes Sar Admiral Odie Hood, Admiral Sirlahn Inglet, Constable General Erl Morgaan, Surgeon General Zig Al Fidel, Commodore Sha Dark, Brigadier Greatjon Humber, Colonel Stonewin Kirkmount, and Colonel Yoseph Sarlin. Krittain's constitutional monarch King Erskine Braemar is dressed as Field Marshal of the Krittish Volunteers. Likewise Prince Abdulay O'Jordaan II of Udalooy is dressed as a Field Marshal of the Ixin Foreign Legion. Even some members of the Xi Charrian delegation boast rank insignia on their carapace, Prelate r'ratak-varattak-n'gavanak, a Vice Admiral in his role as Chief Executive Officer of Haor Chall Engineering, Deans ak and 'radathak-davasak-dannarak, Rear Admirals in their own roles.

%tDruze's crew don't really have "formal dress," but he has enough sense not to attend in 'leather kilt and fur stole,' as was formerly his habit. The babble of various languages seems to please him, everyone going on about this-engineering-conference or that-hyperdrive-research-think-tank. Whatever he lacks in medals and sashes he makes up for in enthusiasm! And, for the occasion, a properly-trimmed beak and a clean jumpsuit. Can't be bungling first impressions, nosir!

%tGrand Moff Elana Tracer, former Presav of the Union, emerges from a sleek, black limo speeder into the drizzle of a Plaxton evening with fellow Moff Etiel te Danaan at her side. The pair hurry across the plaza and into the lobby. "That was fun!" The petite brunette laughs as she shakes water droplets off of her wrap and Etiel takes it from her.%r%t"If you say so..." The desert raised Jedi Etiel looks highly sceptical of Elana's assessement of 'fun' but then smiles and kisses her cheek before taking their outwear to check it before they both head into the Council chamber.%r%t"Wow..." Elana takes in the renovated space with her cool blue gaze. "This is nice! I'll have to compliment Crestian when we see him after." Her friend, having never seen the before has no reference for the changes but seeing her impressed makes him happy. Tracer's red painted lips part in a smile and the pair lets an usher escort them to their seats up front. The former Ace A-Wing pilot feels a flutter of excitement seeing all of the hardware on display and Etiel has to keep her from walking into a tall Sluissi and she issues sheepish apologies. Turning to find Vichten after the Sluissi slithers away, she sees his garb and, after Etiel nods to the Inquisitor, Tracer declares, "Axel! Polished all the medals I see. No one told me that our vintage greens were encouraged or else I would've obliged!"

%tCountless high profile officers of the Caspian Defence Force are among the guests present. Defence Group Charros is particularly well represented including the 3rd Fleet's commanding officer, the Yari Fleet Admiral Loknar Orio, Admiral Shan-Luke Pickart of CMS Reliant, Fleet Captain charak of CMS Charros, and Captain Mallagarra of CMS Cormorant. The 3rd Fleet's Defence Group Mikassa is represented as well with Rear Admiral 'ak-'lak of CMS Tenacious, the highest ranking Xi Charrian field officer, and cousin to Councillor ak and Fleet Captain Thore-Edo of CMS Tudedythiceon.%r%tThe 1st Fleet is represented by its commanding officer Fleet Admiral Cordelia Shale along with Captain Upari Calicona of CMS Astute, Captain Chuunharr of CMS Anchorron, and Captain Sadim Gnik of CMS Neosuhas. While the 2nd Fleet is represented by Fleet Admiral Davik Nord of KMS King Liam I, the former Commander of the Krittish Navy and Commodore Caalbar of CMS Tempest.%r%tThe Caspian 4th Fleet is probably best represented of all. In additional to the three members of the Union Council who serve as commanders in the fleet, the 4th Fleet is represented by Vice Admiral Kath Samba of CMS Renaissance of Innovation, Rear Admiral Demetria of CMS Daring, Commodore Terina Manoff of CMS Hurricane, Fleet Captain Drorq of the Dying Sun, and Fleet Captain Shelshasa, of the Tethys.%r%tThe Caspian Starfighter Corps and Union Marines are both well represented among the guests. Security for the event itself is provided by the Union Marines led by General Janus Tarasar and Colonel V1C3, the latter wearing his marine uniform for once.

%tAxel smiles to Tracer. "Well, I didn't know what to wear, and this just seemed fitting. I know a guy in Noques that is amazing with boots, and a jeweler who's great with brass," he says with a smile. Still though, there was an odd sense of pride on the man's face and in his posture as he stood there in uniform, and considering this was one of the only uniforms he didn't modify in some fashion, he must have at least some sense of purpose when it came to his home.%r%t"You look quite fetching though in that dress, definitely more flattering than most any dress uniform," Axel adds with a smile. "At some other point, I need to have a chat with you, but now is neither the time nor place."%r%tThe man looks about, his eyes lingering on the pilots of the starfighter corps. "When did they start recruiting children to be pilots?" he asks Tracer. "They all look like babies, or have we just gotten old?"

%tShaking the rain off of her jacket before she drapes it over one arm, Lynae takes a long glance around, curiosity more than anything else evident in her expression, as she moves through the assembled crowd. The murmur of voices, languages, dialects, snippets of conversation creates a sea of sound to move through. A faint, ever so slightly amused, smile curves her lips but doesn't quite light her eyes. Familiar faces, familiar voices, and here of all places. There's also a touch of nostalgia in that smile.

%tCaspian civil society is well presented tonight as well. Crestian's Firrerreo wife is in the front row in an azure dress, while their daughter Atsvara wears an obsidian coloured suit which looks just a few notes off of being the same as the uniforms worn by the crew of the Leviathan.%r%tCrestian's best friend, the fashion designer Zubindi Hakoon has a prominent seat as well. He played a large role in arranging the production of the tapestries along with Councillors Satine Anatinae and Andra Eleria. The latter's niece, Zubindi's ward Veruca Violet is in attendence as well.%r%tMembers of the business community, diplomats, local government officials, and foreign guests round out the council chambers. There is not a free seat to be had as the speech had been touted to the guests as a landmark one.

%tDruze nods, firmly. "That's the advantage of our breeding-methods, Commander-- you can't aim a turbolaser or outfly a grouchy superior officer from inside an egg-sac. 'Too slow? Bam-- eaten by your siblings on the way to devour your mother's corpse!'" He pounds the table in a fit of nationalistic pride! He pauses to reflect whether he's laughing at Nature's pitiless competition, purples slightly with shame. He sits.%r%tDruze *empties his champagne-flute,* theatrically. "Sorry. Had one too many."

%tThe young socialite and occasional model known solely as Nara enters a few moments behind the Grand Moff and her Jedi escort. Pausing long enough to yield up the CU Naval greatcoat that serves as her wrap, she takes a few steps to one side just clear of the entryway to look over the festivities. Dressed in a stylized bolero jacket in Caspian green made to resemble a fighter pilot's jacket, complete with Union patches, worn open over an azure leotard over black tights and cuffed ankle boots not too dissimilar from a pilot's ankle boots. Crossed belts of matte silver metal plates mated to a single heavy gold buckle add an additional touch of high martial style to her outfit. Her long, straight pale blonde hair is worn in a thick braid, and a pair of chromed flight goggles are worn around her neck, mating oddly with the Naval Flight Officer's cap perched atop her head.%r%tOnce she has her wits about her, she decides to mingle, making sure to have a smile for everyone. Alternatively idly twirling or slapping her palm with a short gilt-tipped swagger stick mildly reminiscent of a riding crop, she makes the rounds of the Caspian pilots first.

%tOnce most of the guests had made it to their seats, Vice Presav Mos rose and moved towards the podium. "On behalf of the entire Crest I want to thank you all for coming today. A few short years ago the Caspia System found itself suffering the greatest tragedies it has ever known in its modern history and the union which bound us together faltered. Today we stand on the pinnacle of a new greater future. This is due in no small measure to the leadership and vision of our Presav Crestian Tarasar. So if you can all give your attention forward and make it to your seats or standing spots if you have not already done so, I yield the floor to my friend and colleague, the Presav."%r%tAs the Vice Presav leaves the podium and the Presav heads towards it they stop to salute one another as they about to pass, before shaking each other's hands, and then finally embracing in a brief hug. Presav Tarasar then takes the podium. "When the Caspian Democratic Union was founded following Endor our industrial and technological community were still effectively backwater. Our early leaders knew how vital it was for us to develop that prowess, and not just in terms of our defence industry, but across all sectors of our economy.%r%tUnder Presav Lyr Dunwell we shot for the stars. Under the Crest Commission Consolidation Charter we heavily invested in FineLine Spaceways, Interspatial Prospecting, NovaCom Cybertronics and Sublight Products Corporation. We consolidated faltering businesses to form Consolidated Air & Surface Engineering. And we formed Caspian Design & Manufacturing Corporand.%r%tThis became known as our Renaissance of Innovation. It was during this renaissance Yaerberron, now known as Dunwelton emerged as a powerhouse of commerce and industry. It was during this renaissance that Kichnar Orbital Station transformed from a drilling rig into a major orbital city and production hub which now boasts a population of ten million. It was during this renaissance we started to build the ships you see on the wall and laid the foundations for those we built later."

%t"Why thank you!" Tracer replies to the former Reservist. "But truly, you earned every one of those. Your service to Caspar...I thank you for it. The Winds may blow us in many directions. But Casohav also gives us strength and draws us home again and again." Her wistful words and sincere gratitude causes Etiel to view Vichten with a newfound respect as his hand rests lightly on Elana's bare shoulder. "Are you as excited as I am to see these new ships in action?" she asks and Etiel rolls his eyes slightly and comment, "This woman. She loves ships."%r%t"But I know what you mean about the pilots. See that one over there?" she asks pointing to a tall lanky young man in a slate green flightsuit as they take their seats. "Very young. That one, my Lon, is seventeen. Seventeen! I know, I know, I flew that young but it came as a shock when he signed up."

%tHaving found a place to stand, somewhere behind some aged soldiers mixed with some merchants and at least one stuffy sounding diplomatic type, Lynae has the majority of her attention on the Presav. She isn't quite capable of not glancing at the exits and the milling crowd, but at least MOST of her attention is on the Presav.

%tAxel smiles to Tracer, "I am personally quite excited about the new ships." He looks to Etiel. "Jedi are forces to be reckoned with, but starships are great equalizers. I have faced Jedi and Sith alike in space, and came out ahead," he says.%r%tThen, Axel's attention follows Tracer's gesturing, "I was flying younger, but mostly tramp freighters, not starfighters. If he's made it into the starfighter corps, then he's definitely inherited his mother's grace and finesse." Axel chuckles. "I never faced you in space, but on the swoop tracks, you were a sheer force of nature."%r%tOf course, Axel's only half paying attention to the podium, all the pomp and circumstance was secondary to the real reason he was here. He was only here for the ships, after all.

%tCrestian takes a moment to take a long sip of water from a glass waiting for him at the podium as he allows the first part of his history lesson to be absorbed. Putting the water down he resumes, "With the early loss of Dunwell, so too did our renaissance fail to quite live up to its potential. The spark of innovation we never truly lit in Anchorron. The spark at Krittain and Kasputin Yar never quite reached its potential. And eventually the spark of innovation here on Caspar was extinguished and sadly so too with it later the spark of the union itself.%r%tThe past four years have been another rebirth for the Union following the tragedies we faced then, so many dead, so many displaced. We have changed a lot but while things have felt new and different, the word innovation never seemed to quite fit. There were no great strides in technology. There was investment for sure, in multiple sectors, but the promise of a new day was only half met.%r%tThat changed when we welcomed the Xi Char into the Union. They too have had to rebuild from past devastations. But, it has been through their work that I can fully say we are now in the midst of our Second Renaissance of Innovation. I know you were expecting a long boring speech from me, but I would like to turn this over to the man of the hour. When future Caspians look back on our Second Renaissance of Innovation the man who perhaps be remembered as the most important contributor to it all Councillor ak."

%tUnder her breath as the VP makes the opening remarks, a very flattered Tracer still manages a blush at her age and station - still self-effacing as always. "All of my kids flew very young. The need for speed is definitely in the blood," she tells Axel soto voce. Elana stands and applauds as Crestian is introduced along with the bulk of the attendees. She smiles proudly at her friend for what's he's achieved in the past few years for Caspia. Her heart will always protect her home.

%tAxel stands and joins the others in applause. "You'll have to excuse me," Axel says as he gets a buzz on his comlink implant. "I promised I'd have some ships in place to gather sensor data for all this, and my crew needs better directions than I can give them without making a scene during the presentation," he says towards Elana. Quietly, he makes his way through the crowd towards the exit so he could chew ass on the comms without so many witnesses. Briefly, he pauses next to Lynae, leaning in to give her a quick peck on the cheek. "Hey Doc," he says with a wink and smile before heading out.

%tLynae rests one hand lightly on Axel's arm in return, "Axel," said in the same low voice, the faint smile on her face warms slightly as she shares a brief moment with her oldest friend. "Dinner, soon," she adds in the same low voice before he continues to move along. Her attention shifts back to the Presav, focusing once more on his words.

%tAs Councillor ak, the three metre tall Xi Charrian, walks to the podium, a round of applause breaks out from the rest of the Union Council which soon spreads to the audience. Crestian side steps from the podium before bowing in front of the man who already stands over a metre taller than him, before saluting him and walking back to his own seat. A spherical droid plated in beryllius and xonolite hovers around him. It is the prototype of the Articulation Conversation Sphere, or 'ac sphere,' his method of translating the clicks of the Xi Charrian language into Galactic Basic Standard.%r%tAs ak begins to speak the, clicks which are unintelligible to most everyone outside the Xi Charrians themselves are broadcast by the sphere, "The Presav is too kind with his words. Three years ago a vision emerged from here on Caspar and the halls of power on Selene. A vision of a better future for my people, a vision to repair the damage done to the dignity of our faith, a vision to restore our legacy. It is on that vision that all our progress is built on today. There are many people to thank for it but most especially Presav Tarasar and Grand Moff Tracer. We long swore off letting humanoids into our faith, but you know those heathens are alright." In the background Crestian along with several other Councillors can be seen laughing at ak's joke.%r%t"Work started out small." There is a brief pause before the projection screen behind the Union Councillors begins to display a large image of the CMS Daring. "While CMS Daring was still in service, the Crest wanted it modernised and the other surviving Darings back in service as well. So before we even finished negotiating the terms of leaving the Imperium and joining the Union we were contracted to lead that effort in cooperation with Lokkai Starworks.%r%tTo borrow a phrase from Basic, it allowed us to get our feet wet. We got to know the peoples of the Union. We got to see up front and close the needs and opportunities for the Caspian Navy. Most of all we now had a power wanting to work with us as true partners who were interested in what we could build for them. I am, of course, talking about starfighters."

%tNara, now seated with the rest of the dignitaries, applauds with vigor in the aftermath of the Presav's speech. She stifles a chuckle at ak's joke with a ladylike hand wrapped in a nerfskin flight glove.%r%tIn Basic, she adds a soft, "The hope and pride in this place, here and now... it's such a warm, beautiful thing. If I could stay in one moment, forever... this would be it." Really, she can't stop smiling.

%tTracer nods to Axel in complete understanding of his need to depart. "Go! Go! We'll talk later," she whisper hoarsely to him. Her attention returns to the presentation. She smiles to see dear ak come up to speak, her friendship with the Xi Charrian being one of the strangest but most fun she'd ever known. When he mentions the starfighters, Tracer joins the surge of applause for the starfighter, adding in a sharp whistle of appreciation for the small but powerful craft that changed her life and gave her purpose so long ago.

%tThe image of CMS Daring leaves the screen and it remains blank for a moment. ak continues to speak, "There are four new designs to show you today, but before I put them up on the screen I want to introduce you to pilots for three of them."%r%tIt is at this moment that three pilots wearing flight suits of the Caspian Starfighter Corps step forward, a Human, a Wookiee, and a Yari. There is a great variation in size between the three. The Human stands less than two metres tall, the Wookiee is closer to two and half metres, while the Yari is roughly three and a half metres tall.%r%tAs the three stand next to ak he remarks, "You probably wouldn't expect Junior Flight Lieutenant Longinus Van Sen, Group Captain Attibecca, and Squadron Commander Semtech Keno to all be pilots. The Union is a diverse place, the galaxy is a diverse place, but most shipwrights focus the designs around humanity. Here today we break that trend. Here the Caspian Union says defence is truly a shared responsibility."

%tNara applauds enthusiastically at the mention of starfighters. Because, really, who wouldn't? They are the most visible part of the armed forces, and the most romanticized. The three pilots get just a little more applause. In Basic, she muses, "I wonder if they use species-specific craft? Or maybe highly customizeable cockpit fittings?"

%tLynae's attention flicks from screen to Presav then skims slowly over the introduced pilots, one eyebrow arching ever so subtly as the contrast and comparison is so calmly evident. Trust Caspar to adapt it's gear and war machines for flexibility.

%tThe projection behind the Union Councillors now begins displaying four starfighter models and as ak talks about each one it is enlarged on the screen compared to the others. "We designed two starfighter classes for use on the Daring-Class and on Kichnar Orbital Station. The first is the Mark IV Vulture-Class Droid Starfighter, the latest version of the ship we are most known for. Complimenting them will be the Guardian-Class Starfighter, the first starfighter design made for use by the Yaries."%r%tak then motions to another Xi Charrian among the Union Councillors. "For planetary defence we have the brand new design of Dean 'radathak-davasak-dannarak, the Vector and Apex classes. She based her design on the Vector Apex symbol of the Union, itself based on Caspar's native Cason Hawk. The Vector-Class is a traditionally sized model for pilots like Lieutenant Van Sen." The ac sphere translates Lieutenant with the traditional Caspian pronunciation of 'leftenant.' Then motion to the Wookie, "The Apex-Class is more in her size."%r%tThe projections disappear again, and the projection screen itself reverts to being a window back out into Plaxton. "Of course, projections do not do them justice." At that moment a Vulture-Class flies by, followed by the Vector-Class, and the Apex-Class, and finally the Guardian-Class. The fly by is fast but then they loop around and repeat with a much slower fly by.

%tAlright, Tracer thinks, Lon cuts a handsome cloth in his flightsuit. The dark-haired lad with just a hint of a mustache over his upper-lip was really starting to look like his father. A sudden flood of sorrow within her causes Etiel concern as he senses it. Devoid of any jealousy over Elana's reverie, he puts a comforting arm around her shoulder and her eyes sparkle watching the display.

%tLynae's eyes are narrowed ever so slightly, a gleam of speculation now evident in her eyes. She shifts the jacket she's holding from one arm to the other, shaking the now-free arm for a moment, attention fixed on the display. Her eyes flick from the display to the pilots, back to the display, and if anything the gleam of speculation actually grows.

%tNoting the red-gowned woman's distress in the middle of cheering for the sleek new fighters passing in review, Nara leans closer to the woman others know as Grand Moff Tracer. "Are you all right, Ma'am?" she asks gently, hesitating to touch her since she's got the man beside her trying to comfort her.

%tAfter the fly by is concluded, ak speaks again, "It was at this point that Sirlahn and I wanted to bust in with the new E5 Baron Droids and IG-240 Hailfire-Class Droid Tanks." There is a long pause before he adds, "We were advised that doing so might induce panic in the audience and create a hazardous situation of people fleeing to the exits. Now that we have given you a bit of warning would it be alright if we show you some projections?"%r%tThe question appears rhetorical as the Sluissi Councillor Sirlahn Inglet who is in charge of SubPro slithers forward and drops two projection devices on the opposition side of where the pilots are standing. Upon hitting the floor they project the latest iteration of the Baron Droids and Hailfire Droid Tanks. Sirlahn briefly joins ak at the podium and remarks, "The Union may not be a large power, but we will defend our borders and worlds with resolve. We will ensure this continues to be a place people fleeing calamity elsewhere can find safe harbour."%r%tSirlahn then presses a button on a remote causing the projections to disappear and he slithers back to seat. ak then motions to another Xi Charrian Councillor, "Prelate r'ratak-varattak-n'gavanak has graciously invited the Caspian Defence Force to host wargames on Charros IV itself and in the Charros System. Tonight is just a taste of what is to come. Starting next week elements of the Caspian 3rd and 4th Fleets, the Union Marines, and various invited guests shall begin a series of wargames to truly showcase our new works. Thank you all for coming tonight. Please enjoy the food and drink."

%tThat speculative gleam in Lynae's eyes has changed to something that's almost - or really quite entirely - gleeful. She applauds, oh yes, she applauds. She's also, clearly, loudly, approving of the announcement of war games.

%tTracer sniffles and then turns to give the woman next to her a wan smile. "I'm fine, thank you." Covering for herself, she indicates the pilots on stage. "Just a proud mama moment."%r%tThen outcome the ground vehicles, much to her surprise. She hadn't heard Crestian mention those. Very impressive.%r%tAs the showcase concludes, Etiel whispers in her ear and makes as quick an exit as he can with the audience now milling about. Tracer watches him leave before standing. She turns again to the young woman who was sitting beside her, as she stands. "Thank you again for your concern." Looking over the other woman's outfit, Tracer cocks her head to one side. It wasn't a dress uniform or any other kind that Tracer can discern. "Your outfit is fantastic, by the way. I'm...you are Caspar military, yes? I'm Elana, by the way."

%tDruze rises, admitting to the nearbys: "I may have a reputation for two ventral fins. And I'd hate to be famous for scratching the paint on--" he *chins* over to the tanks and the walking murder-droids. "-- even as a doctor, I don't think I make enough to cover it!"

%tWith the presentation over, there are so many people Crestian wants to go up to. People he wants to thank for their work. People he wants to interest in the next stage. People he is hoping they have impressed. But as a family man, his first stop after the success of the announcement is to the front row where his wife and daughter are. As various guests stand, be it in applause or a mad dash for to nosh on snacks and get free liquor, Crestian picks up his daughter a moment before setting her back down. She is almost ten, probably too cool for that. "Thank you for coming Atsvara. I know how busy your schedule has been lately."%r%tThe girl giggles, "Well you kept telling me I needed to get some fresh air Tevas. So naturally I thought a crowded room full of half of Caspar was the place to start. I am disappointed we didn't get to see the actual Droids. I wanted to see the Droids. You should have brought out the Droids." She does add with affectation of frustration, "But I suppose I can forgive this based on these 'safety' concerns."%r%tCrestian chuckles a bit, "Some of us can't afford to just unleash chaos and see how it goes. Though I suppose that is exactly what we have taught you to do." He looks to his wife for a moment, a bit of pride on his face before lookings back towards other VIPs, making his way towards Grand Moff Tracer.

%t"The young man on the stage is your son, then?" Nara asks, her smile widening. "You've every reason to be proud... he's quite handsome, and he seems to be well positioned to make a name for himself. And we must cherish our men and women in uniform. Would that I had half their courage and sense of purpose." In Basic, of course.%r%tShe shakes her head gracefully at the question. "No, Ma'am... I'm here for the party, and to show my support for the troops. It's wonderful to meet you, Elana. I'm Nara." And she offers her hand and a warm smile, nodding to the Jedi as he leaves.

%tElana takes the woman's offered hand with the firm but gentle grip of someone who is in charge but not a tyrant. "Very nice to meet you, as well, Nara. And a party and patriotism are perfect reasons to be here tonight." As a server passes by with trays of bubbly stuff, Tracer snatches two glasses and offers one to Nara before sipping her own.%r%tNoticing the Presav managing to cut a swath from his family through his well wishers, she raises her drink in his direction. "Bravo, Tarasar!" she calls out to him, then levels her sardonic ice-blue gaze at him. "Well done! I think I'm not even angry anymore about the whole giving my son one of the most dangerous jobs in the galaxy thing."

%tThe Presav chuckles a little as he approaches Elana, "Well do note I put him in the room as prop....well ak put him the room as prop, someone else did the really dangerous work of flying the Vector-Class through Plaxton." Crestian looks around the room, "Get over here ak and say hello to Elana." ak of course is easy enough to find in a crowd based on his height, but there were in fact dozens of Xi Charrians in the room. Fortunately ak is nearby, practically surrounded the adulation of various appreciative admirals, engineers, and pilots. He makes his apologies to them as he makes his way over to the Presav and former Presav.%r%tCrestian then looks to Nara. He smiles approvingly at her outfit, "Well miss you seem to have completely nailed it in the fashion department. If you aren't already working for Zubindi I must expect you will be soon." His mannerisms are just a bit off as if he is trying to convey something deeper while not seeming to be.%r%tFor Atsvara's part she is far more interested in talking to Longinus and make her way to the young pilot. He also seems to have acquired a few fans, be it from the boyish good looks, the recognition of his name, or just the thrill everyone has at starfighters. Atsvara is able to effectively clear a path to him through sheer determination and zero fucks given about the people standing in her way. Standing on the tips of her toes she remarks, "It is sooo good to see you Lon. It has been too long...." She pauses, frustrated be the inadvertant sound repetition.

%t"Thank you so much!" Nara replies, accepting the offered glass in one hand and the handclasp with the other. "That's so thoughtful of you. And it's very important to show our appreciation for our citizen-soldiers, no matter their ages. We owe them so much." Once more in Basic.

%tShe smiles at the Presav, executing a graceful curtsey in spite of her lack of skirts. "The great Mister Zubindi is kind enough to let me model for him now and again. Actually, my belt and jacket are his handiwork, and he suggested the boots and officer's cap. And the colors, now that I think of it. Really, any day I get to wear a Zubindi original is a great day, and all the more so for a good cause. By the way, your speech was brilliant. Would that I had that kind of patriotism and presence. I might be something besides a pretty face." Accompanied by an impish smile for Presav and Grand Moff alike.

%t"I still remember my first few weeks in a squadron doing an -unauthorized- fly by over the beach for Salonika's wedding," Tracer reminisces fondly. "He was /soooo/ mad but hey, my CO is the one who planned it so..." She shrugs impishly. Noticing her progeny being chatted up by several pretty young things her eyes close briefly as she pictures his future of love and children of his own in a flash. Her dark lashes flutter open again, her good humor not flagging.%r%tFor Lon's part, despite enjoying all of the attractive attention, the young pilot quickly ignores them all when Atsvara approaches. The teenager gets down on one knee to meet her eye to eye. "I know!!" Being the youngest of Tracer's three children he knew how important it was to give younger people proper attention. Teasing the girl he says, "Like...I think the last time was...beating you at swoop racing behind mom's house on Selene?"%r%tAt the mention of Zubindi Tracer exclaims, "Of course, Nara!" And yes, Elana gets Crestian's implication. "Oh don't be modes Nara. I can tell that there's more to you than looks," she remarks to the model with a quiet wink before ak reaches them.%r%t"ak my man, really fantastic looking product there," Elana says. "We really need to look at adaptive configurations for Imperium fighters now that our forces are no long strictly human."

%tak would be smiling from ear to ear were his body configured differently, "Never in my wildest dreams could I have imagined how much interest there would in our work. I just got back from seeing Johanna, and Kipran, and Cricket on Kessel. Renaissance of Innovation indeed!" The ac sphere does a passable job in conveying the emotions that go with the Xi Charrian's words. After all he did design the thing.%r%tCrestian for his own part is probably grinning enough for them both, "It is really a triumph for us all, but especially ak." He looks to Nara in specific, "We need to get Zubindi to put ak in a fashion show. Caption the 'modern Caspian man' or something like that. I mean according to the polls ak is actually the most popular member of the Union Council, he even has Zaalbacca beat."%r%tAtsvara does smiles as Lon kneels down to give her attention, though it quickly turns to a small smirk when he mentions the swoop racing, "Well you have a lot more practice. I suppose you will beat me at starfighter flying now. No one has let me fly on yet. I mean I am almost ten, it is time."

%t"I'm touched that you remember me from Mr. Zubindi's catalogue... I'm hopeful he'll let me do a little work for him next season, too," Nara replies, blushing faintly. "But all I can properly claim credit for is good genetics, and even that's not my doing. Perhaps he'll teach me a little of design someday, so I can be of some real use to him, and to the House."%r%tHer smile turns brightly enthusiastic at the Presav's suggestion. "Oh, that's /briliant/! And he so richly deserves it, for all he's done for the Union, and he's a brilliant designer in his own right! And now I can only wonder if there is synergy between fashion design and starship design... or maybe that's just my love of wearing beautiful clothes and watching fast ships. I must think on that sometime."
